name: Yakut
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Yakut is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Yakut (Sakha, саха тыла) is a Siberian Turkic language of the Sakha Republic, Russia, written since 1939 in an extended CYRILLIC ALPHABET which adds the letters ҕ ҥ ө һ ү and the digraphs дь нь to the Russian inventory; it has vowel harmony and phonemic long vowels/diphthongs written by doubling. INPUT CONTRACT: Cyrillic Sakha text (Unicode, NFC); earlier Novgorodov and Latin (Yañalif) orthographies are out of scope. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode yaku1245), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode yaku1245.

ancestors.notes: Turkic — the classification node this language sits under.
